title: quercus xanthotricha
intro: Quercus xanthotricha, also known as the yellow-haired oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to Mexico and Central America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains quercetin and other tannins that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus xanthotricha include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The bark is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
